title: Spanakopita

overview.description: A traditional Greek spinach and feta pie baked in crisp filo pastry. Rich enough to be satisfying while still feeling fresh and light.

ingredients: 1kg frozen chopped spinach, thawed and thoroughly squeezed dry
method: Heat the olive oil in a frying pan over a medium heat.
Cook the spring onions for 3–4 minutes until softened.
Combine the spinach, feta, spring onions, dill, parsley and eggs in a large bowl.
Brush a baking dish with a little melted butter.
Lay in one sheet of filo pastry and brush lightly with butter.
Spread the filling evenly over the pastry.
Cover with the remaining filo sheets, brushing each with melted butter.
Tuck the edges neatly into the dish.
Score the top layers into serving portions without cutting right through.
Bake at 180°C fan (200°C conventional) for 40–45 minutes until crisp and deep golden.
Allow to cool for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Spanakopita has been enjoyed throughout Greece for centuries and belongs to a family of savoury pies known as 'pites'.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to prepare spinach properly, work with delicate filo pastry and create crisp pastry layers.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fantastic vegetarian centrepiece that introduces filo pastry while making excellent use of frozen spinach.
chapman-tips: Squeeze as much water as possible from the spinach before mixing the filling.
Keep the filo covered with a damp tea towel while working to prevent it drying out.
Brush each layer lightly—you don't need to soak the pastry with butter.
Scoring before baking makes serving much easier.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze either before or after baking for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in the oven or air fryer to restore the crisp pastry.
